relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Messiah's suffering forms a mind no longer ruled by misalignment.
Remaining life turns from human desire toward Divine will.
The old flood of excess is enough.
Others may slander the changed life.
All give account to the Judge of living and dead.
Good news reaches even the dead in mystery.
Each grace-gift serves the many-colored grace of Divine.
Speech and service become Divine-strengthened glorifying.
Sharing Messiah's path opens joy in glory.
Reproach in Messiah's name is met by Spirit resting.
Do not confuse suffering for wrongdoing with suffering for Messiah.
No shame belongs to bearing Messiah's name.
The path of the just is sober and searching.
Those suffering while doing good entrust life to the faithful Creator.

contemplative_note: 1 Peter 4 is grace under pressure: sober prayer, earnest love, hospitality, gift-service, and entrusting life to the faithful Creator without glorifying harm.
